framework.name: Software Factory Security Framework
framework.description: A strategic framework for scaling security capabilities while improving business outcomes
framework.author: Julie Davila

quadrants.name: Visionaries
quadrants.strategy: Automate from the start, build scaling foundations

quadrants.name: Leaders
quadrants.strategy: Optimize existing, build platform effects, competitive advantage

quadrants.name: Niche Players
quadrants.strategy: Choose intentional simplicity OR prepare for growth

quadrants.name: Challengers
quadrants.strategy: Stabilize first, hybrid approach, realistic timelines

universal_stewardship.description: Five areas requiring attention regardless of organizational position
universal_stewardship.priority_note: Supply Chain became #1 priority in 2017 due to adversary evolution to automated discovery at internet scale

areas.name: Supply Chain Stewardship
areas.focus: Managing security risks from all external dependencies and third-party components throughout their lifecycle

areas.name: Process Stewardship
areas.focus: Security embedded throughout development lifecycle with continuous validation and rapid feedback loops

areas.name: Runtime Stewardship
areas.focus: Maintaining security and reliability of systems in production with proactive monitoring and rapid response

areas.name: Third-Party Stewardship
areas.focus: Managing security risks from integrated services, platforms, and vendors throughout operational lifecycle

areas.name: Continuous Learning
areas.focus: Evolving security practices based on experience, incidents, and changing threat landscape

investment_portfolio.description: Framework for categorizing security work and making investment decisions

categories.name: BAU (Business-as-Usual)
categories.description: Manual security work that scales linearly with growth

categories.name: Scaling Investments
categories.description: Capabilities that reduce manual effort exponentially
categories.strategy: Primary investment focus past crisis point

categories.name: Platform Effects
categories.description: Benefits that serve both internal and customer software factories
categories.value: Multiplicative impact across organization and customers

scaling_crisis.definition: The inevitable moment when demand for security services grows exponentially while team capacity grows linearly
scaling_crisis.signal: Security reviews blocking releases, team burnout, months-long backlogs
scaling_crisis.response: Shift investment from BAU to scaling capabilities

contextual_modifiers.description: Six factors that significantly affect strategy implementation

modifiers.name: Attack Landscape Maturity
modifiers.impact: Accelerates need for automation; manual processes become existential vulnerabilities
high_maturity_characteristics: Bug bounty techniques at internet scale

modifiers.name: Supply Chain Complexity
modifiers.impact: May require Leaders-level tools regardless of base quadrant

modifiers.name: Regulatory Constraints
modifiers.impact: Increases BAU burden; may delay quadrant progression

modifiers.name: Change Capacity
modifiers.impact: Affects transition speed and scaling investment success probability

modifiers.name: Relationship Health
levels: Strategic: Security as enabler and partner

framework_relationships.description: How SF² integrates with other security frameworks
framework_relationships.principle: SF² is strategic overlay that guides sequencing, prioritization, and sustainability

frameworks.name: NIST SSDF
frameworks.sf2_provides: Implementation strategy for sustainable resourcing and scaling

frameworks.name: OWASP SAMM
frameworks.sf2_provides: Which maturity levels to pursue and how fast to progress

frameworks.name: BSIMM
frameworks.sf2_provides: Investment priorities for activities based on organizational positioning

frameworks.name: OWASP ASVS
frameworks.sf2_provides: Risk-based ASVS subset vs comprehensive implementation guidance

ai_integration.description: Guidance for using AI tools with SF² framework

key_principles: Supply Chain Stewardship is #1 priority since 2017 adversary evolution
Constrain BAU activities, invest in scaling capabilities
Two-axis transformation is high-risk; sequence carefully
Appropriate security depends on organizational position
Framework provides strategic overlay for other security frameworks
Realistic timelines: Challengers need 3-5 years for transformation

target_audience.use_case: Strategic decision-making about positioning, investment, and transformation
target_audience.value_proposition: Context-appropriate guidance with realistic timelines and actionable steps
